name: Debugging X-Ray
description: A polished product screenshot scanned by an animated x-ray band that reveals the underlying component structure as glowing devtools-style bboxes and accessibility outlines. Drag the pointer, or let it auto-sweep — exactly the feeling of opening the inspector on production.

fields.label: Heading
fields.default: See every render, every leak, every layout shift.

fields.label: Subheading
fields.default: An x-ray for your product. Drag across the screenshot to peel back the polished UI and read the component tree, accessibility outlines, and render-cost hotspots underneath.

fields.label: On click
fields.description: URL to navigate to when the CTA is clicked. From code you can also pass a function — same prop name, type-detected at runtime.

fields.label: Column 1
fields.default:
Production-grade visibility

Every paint, every prop, every accessibility violation — surfaced as you scan.

fields.label: Column 2
fields.default:
Zero overhead

Runs in production. Streams to your dashboard. Slows nothing down.

fields.label: Content
fields.default:
See everything.

X-ray your product in real time.

fields.label: Screenshot (PNG/JPG/WebP/AVIF)
fields.description: The product screenshot the x-ray scans across. Dashboards, IDEs and admin UIs look best — the more visible structure, the better the reveal.

fields.label: Screenshot size
fields.description: Scale of the screenshot plane. 1.0 fits the smaller axis; 1.05 pushes it slightly larger for a confident hero presence.

fields.label: Horizontal position
fields.description: Shift the screenshot left or right so the heading can sit opposite. 0 = centred.

fields.label: Screen tilt
fields.description: Tilt the screenshot toward (-) or away from (+) the camera. Tiny tilts read as 'product card' rather than 'flat banner'.

fields.label: Scan mode
fields.description: How the x-ray band moves across the screenshot. Both mode lets it auto-sweep while idle, then jumps to follow your pointer when you move.

fields.label: Auto sweep speed
fields.description: Speed of the auto-sweep, in screen-widths per second. 0.3–0.5 reads as 'thoughtful inspection'; above 1.0 feels frantic.

fields.label: Scan band width
fields.description: Width of the reveal band as a fraction of the screenshot width. 0.25–0.4 reads as 'a tool scanning'; >0.7 reveals the whole image at once.

fields.label: Accent colour
fields.description: Tint of the x-ray overlay (devtools blue, cyan, magenta, lime — pick the one that reads against your screenshot).

fields.label: X-ray source
fields.description: What gets revealed under the scan band. Procedural draws devtools-style bboxes + heatmap in-shader (no extra asset needed). Image swaps that for a second image you provide — a wireframe, blueprint, code-tree screenshot, schematic — perfectly aligned under the polished screenshot.

fields.label: X-ray image (PNG/JPG/WebP/AVIF)
fields.description: The image revealed under the scan band. Use the same aspect ratio as your screenshot for clean alignment (cover-fit). Leave blank to fall back to the procedural overlay.

fields.label: Overlay style
fields.description: Bounding boxes are devtools-style outlines; the heatmap glows brighter where the underlying UI region is busier (luminance-driven). Both layered together reads as 'profiler + inspector at once'.

fields.label: Overlay density
fields.description: Density of the procedural bounding-box grid. 0.4–0.7 reads as 'detailed but legible'; pushing higher fills the band with tighter nested boxes.

fields.label: Pointer parallax
fields.description: How much the camera drifts with the pointer. 0 = locked; 0.5 = subtle product-shot parallax; 1.0 = playful.

fields.label: Background (top)
fields.description: Top of the radial backdrop behind the screenshot.

fields.label: Transparent background
fields.description: Drop the backdrop so the hero composes onto a page section below it.
